---
title: "Rollout Storage And Invalidity Contract"
phase: thesis
audience: developer
status: planned
owner: code
format: html
---

# Rollout Storage And Invalidity Contract {#rollout-storage-contract}

This page drafts the storage contract for large rollout and $Q_H$ datasets. It
is a schema target, not a writer implementation. The purpose is to keep rollout
generation, invalidity handling, target records, and fitted $Q_H$ tensors
compatible before any large ASE sequence data is written.

The contract is Zarr-first for dense numeric data and small columnar tables. It
should track the [Zarr v3 core model](https://zarr-specs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/v3/core/index.html)
for groups, arrays, metadata, and attributes, while allowing compact JSON or
JSONL sidecars for dictionaries and manifest text that is awkward to encode as
dense arrays. The root store attribute should name the ARIA-NBV schema, schema
version, Zarr format version, creation time, code revision, and the source
offline-store version.

## Store Shape {#rollout-store-shape}

The draft store has one root group per generated dataset:

```text
rollouts.zarr/
  attrs
  metadata/
  dictionaries/
  splits/
  lineage/
  mesh_refs/
  targets/
  rollouts/
  steps/
  candidates/
  q_h/
  diagnostics/
```

`metadata/` records schema version, generation manifest, code/config hashes,
field-retention policy, and reason-code dictionary. `dictionaries/` stores
string ids such as scenes, snippets, policies, configs, target classes, and
rollout ids when implementation chooses integer dictionary encoding. Numeric
arrays should use stable row ids so tables can be joined without relying on
array order alone.

The canonical row order is:

1. `target_row_id` indexes target records.
2. `rollout_row_id` indexes one rollout chain for one root state and target.
3. `step_row_id` indexes one horizon step inside a rollout chain.
4. `candidate_row_id` indexes one sampled candidate inside the full candidate
   shell for one step.

Candidate order is part of the label contract. `shell_index`, validity masks,
oracle labels, predicted scores, selected actions, rendered depths, point
clouds, and $Q_H$ tensors must remain aligned through these row ids.

## Lineage {#rollout-lineage}

Lineage is mandatory and must be rejectable. A store that mixes incompatible
lineage should be treated as multiple datasets or invalid input.

Required lineage fields include:

- dataset schema id and version;
- ASE scene id, snippet id, source shard uri, and source shard digest where
  available;
- source VIN/offline-store version, manifest hash, sample-index hash, and split
  manifest hash;
- package git revision or source tree digest;
- candidate generator config hash, oracle config hash, rollout config hash,
  target protocol version, and invalidity reason-code version;
- rollout policy name, horizon, branch factor, beam width, random seed, and
  branch schedule id where applicable;
- model checkpoint hash and feature/extractor config hash when learned scores
  or EVL features are stored;
- $Q_H$ target horizon, discount, return semantics, and bootstrap policy id
  when $Q_H$ tensors are materialized.

The root `lineage/rollout_index` table should hold one row per rollout chain:
`rollout_row_id`, `rollout_id`, `chain_id`, `scene_id`, `snippet_id`,
`target_row_id`, `split`, `root_pose_world`, configured horizon and budgets,
policy id, seed, termination reason, runtime summary, invalid-action summary,
and final cumulative metrics. `root_pose_world` is mandatory because rollout
diagnostics need an explicit root transform to audit whether candidate poses
share the same scene world frame as ASE, GT mesh, EFM voxels, and OBB layers.

## Mesh References And Target Crops {#rollout-mesh-targets}

Full ASE meshes are external references. The rollout store must not duplicate
full scene meshes. `mesh_refs/scene_mesh_index` should record:

- scene id and mesh role, such as `ase_gt_full` or `scene_crop`;
- URI or relative path under the data root;
- content hash, mesh version, unit convention, and coordinate frame;
- optional crop bounds or preprocessing hash;
- vertex/face counts and downsampling policy, if a derived mesh is used.

Target records are stored separately from full meshes. `targets/target_index`
should include:

- `target_row_id`, scene id, snippet id, and target id;
- target protocol version, for example V0 GT input or V1 observed input with
  GT evaluation;
- actor-visible target source, class id/name, confidence, observed OBB pose and
  extents, projected area, semidense support, EVL support, and pose-relative
  target features;
- matched GT target id, GT OBB pose and extents, match score, IoU, class match,
  and match-failure reason;
- `target_valid_mask`, `target_invalid_reason_bitset`, and primary invalid
  reason;
- target-crop row id and crop policy metadata.

High-detail target mesh crops may be embedded once per target because target
RRI depends on the crop. Store them as ragged arrays with offsets, not as a copy
per candidate:

- `targets/crop_vertices`, `targets/crop_vertex_offsets`;
- `targets/crop_faces`, `targets/crop_face_offsets`;
- crop source mesh hash, crop bounds, margin, face-retention rule, and units.

Empty target crops are invalid target records, not scene-level fallback labels.

## Candidate Tables {#rollout-candidate-tables}

`steps/step_index` stores state-level rollout rows: `step_row_id`,
`rollout_row_id`, `step_index`, current pose, accumulated view count, path
length, cumulative target RRI, cumulative scene RRI, runtime, number of sampled
candidates, number of valid candidates, and invalid reason histogram.

`candidates/candidate_index` stores the full sampled shell for every step:

- `candidate_row_id`, `step_row_id`, `rollout_row_id`, `step_index`, and
  `shell_index`;
- candidate pose as world-from-camera and relative-to-root fields, with frame
  names in attributes;
- candidate strategy id, sampler mixture id, sampler probability, and optional
  parent branch id;
- `candidate_valid_mask`, `actor_action_mask`, `oracle_label_mask`,
  `q_train_mask`, `padded_mask`, `selected_mask`, and
  `heavy_diag_available_mask`;
- `invalid_reason_bitset`, `primary_invalid_reason`, and optional secondary
  reason count;
- scene RRI, target RRI, accuracy/completeness deltas, target-visible fraction,
  and oracle runtime when labels exist;
- one-step model score, uncertainty/calibration fields, and checkpoint hash
  when learned scores exist;
- selected action rank fields such as selected shell index, selected compact
  valid index, oracle rank, and model rank.

Invalid candidates stay in the table so the full-shell order is replayable.
They are masked out of action selection and $Q_H$ targets instead of being
encoded as the lowest RRI bin.

## Invalidity Contract {#rollout-invalidity-contract}

Invalidity is a constraint label. It is not low reconstruction quality. The
store should carry both boolean masks and reason codes:

- `candidate_valid_mask`: true only when the candidate passes required geometry,
  target, oracle, and evidence checks for the selected protocol.
- `actor_action_mask`: candidates an actor or scorer may choose without seeing
  oracle-only information.
- `oracle_label_mask`: candidates with valid scene/target oracle labels.
- `q_train_mask`: candidates usable as $Q_H$ targets for the configured
  horizon and target protocol.
- `target_valid_mask`: target record has a valid actor-visible input and
  matched GT evaluation crop for the protocol.
- `padded_mask`: false data inserted only to fill dense tensors.

Reason codes are versioned bit positions. A draft initial dictionary is:

| Bit | Code | Meaning |
|---:|---|---|
| 0 | `VALID` | No invalid reason. |
| 1 | `POSE_NONFINITE` | Pose contains NaN or infinity. |
| 2 | `POSE_OUT_OF_EXTENT` | Candidate center is outside supported scene or snippet bounds. |
| 3 | `CAMERA_OUT_OF_EXTENT` | Camera frustum violates configured camera/scene support. |
| 4 | `COLLISION_MESH` | Camera center or body intersects the mesh. |
| 5 | `CLEARANCE_TOO_SMALL` | Candidate is closer to geometry than the configured clearance. |
| 6 | `PATH_SEGMENT_COLLISION` | Motion segment to the candidate violates collision/clearance rules. |
| 7 | `FRUSTUM_OUT_OF_BOUNDS` | Frustum support is outside the trusted volume. |
| 8 | `DEPTH_NO_HIT` | Renderer produced no valid mesh hits. |
| 9 | `DEPTH_TOO_SPARSE` | Renderer produced too few valid depth pixels. |
| 10 | `BACKPROJECT_EMPTY` | Valid depth did not produce usable world points. |
| 11 | `CANDIDATE_DUPLICATE` | Candidate duplicates an existing shell pose under tolerance. |
| 12 | `SAMPLER_RULE_REJECTED` | Candidate failed a named sampler rule. |
| 13 | `TARGET_NOT_ACTOR_VISIBLE` | V1 target input is not actor-visible or eligible. |
| 14 | `TARGET_GT_UNMATCHED` | Observed target could not be matched to GT evaluation. |
| 15 | `TARGET_CROP_EMPTY` | GT target crop has no usable mesh surface. |
| 16 | `TARGET_SUPPORT_TOO_LOW` | Target has insufficient observed, semidense, or EVL support. |
| 17 | `TARGET_VISIBILITY_TOO_LOW` | Candidate does not see enough of the target for the protocol. |
| 18 | `SEMIDENSE_SUPPORT_TOO_LOW` | Candidate or state lacks required semidense support. |
| 19 | `EVL_EVIDENCE_MISSING` | Required EVL evidence or checkpoint provenance is absent. |
| 20 | `MESH_REFERENCE_MISSING` | Required mesh reference or hash is unavailable. |
| 21 | `ORACLE_DISTANCE_FAILED` | Oracle distance/RRI computation failed. |
| 22 | `CANDIDATE_ORDER_GUARD_FAILED` | A row-alignment guard failed before serialization. |
| 23 | `RUNTIME_ERROR` | Required generation failed with an explicit runtime error. |

Bit `0` is the all-clear value, not a bit to combine with failures. Additional
codes may be appended, but existing meanings should not be reused.

## Optional Heavy Diagnostics {#rollout-heavy-diagnostics}

Heavy diagnostics are opt-in and should be stored only for selected actions,
retained chains, or explicitly sampled audit rows:

- rendered candidate depth and depth-valid mask;
- rendered face-index image;
- selected or retained candidate point cloud in world coordinates;
- target-cropped current points and candidate points;
- collision segments and clearance samples;
- Rerun `.rrd` artifact references;
- per-candidate OBB, voxel, or EVL support summaries.

The presence of heavy diagnostics is not a training prerequisite. Missing heavy
diagnostics should be represented by availability masks, not by recomputing or
guessing from ad hoc fields.

## Q_H Tensors {#rollout-qh-tensors}

The columnar candidate table is canonical. `q_h/` is a padded tensor view for
finite-candidate training and evaluation. It should be derivable from the
candidate and step tables and should include row-id backlinks:

- `state_step_row_id[S]`;
- `candidate_row_id[S, C_max]`;
- `valid_action_mask[S, C_max]`;
- `invalid_reason_bitset[S, C_max]`;
- `target_row_id[S]`;
- `selected_candidate_index[S]`;
- `one_step_target_rri[S, C_max]`;
- `one_step_scene_rri[S, C_max]`;
- `q_target_target_rri[S, H_max, C_max]`;
- `q_target_scene_rri[S, H_max, C_max]` as a diagnostic;
- `bootstrap_next_step_row_id[S, C_max]` when a materialized branch exists,
  otherwise `-1`;
- `terminal_mask[S, C_max]`;
- `discount[H_max]` and return-semantics attributes.

Invalid and padded candidates should contain `NaN` targets and false masks.
$Q_H$ predicts bounded cumulative target RRI over finite candidate sets. Motion,
rule, or scalar invalidity penalties are later ablations and should not be
mixed into the first target-RRI tensor unless the return-semantics attribute is
changed.

## Retention Policy {#rollout-retention-policy}

Default stores should retain lineage, target records, candidate tables, masks,
reason codes, compact metrics, and $Q_H$ target tensors. They should reference
full meshes externally, embed target crops once per target, and omit heavy
per-candidate diagnostics unless explicitly requested. This keeps the dataset
trainable, auditable, and storage-bounded while preserving the ability to
reject mixed or invalid lineage.
